_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX state-legislature batch: janis-holt (R HD-18), lois-kolkhorst (R SD-18), ellen-troxclair (R HD-19), roland-gutierrez (D SD-19), juan-chuy-hinojosa (D SD-20). Evidence-first; every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence. Candidacy correction: Hinojosa NOT retiring (mistaken identity w/ ex-Cong. Ruben Hinojosa); seat up 2028.

profile.background: Freshman R (89th Leg, took office Jan 2025); primaried out moderate R Ernest Bailes in 2024. Former public-school teacher, Silsbee ISD board president, business co-owner; ex-RPT SREC committeewoman.

janis-holt@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (autopilot). Hardline-conservative freshman, 89th Leg only. Aggregates: Texas Alliance for Life 96%, Texas Values Action 91% (Faith & Family Champion), Texans for Fiscal Responsibility 91 (Pro-Taxpayer). Authored HB2062 (biological-sex private spaces / bathroom bill), coauthored HB3399 (ban gender-affirming care for minors), HB733 (citizenship verification for residential property purchase), HB668 (LTC renewal), HB3142 (armed school marshals), HB1443/HB2000 (child-grooming/sexualized-content penalties). No cross-party deviation found; documented positions uniformly conservative. Religion: Christian-active, no named denomination found. Still running 2026 (R primary 3/3/26).

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 96% (2025); voted for SB31 Life of the Mother Act and the pro-life slate.

1.note: Voted for HB7 enforcing TX near-total abortion ban (mail-order abortion-drug enforcement).

2.note: No public position found on embryonic/IVF/chimeric research.

3.note: No public position found on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: No evidence of PP/NARAL/EMILY's List money; pro-life-endorsed Republican.

0.note: No explicit man-woman marriage statement found.

1.note: No same-sex marriage / civil-union vote found.

4.note: Texas Values 91% Champion; HB2062 restricts gender ideology in public spaces.

0.note: Strong school-choice/ESA support; 'parents deserve the right to find the best educational situation... public, private, homeschool, charter.'

1.note: Coauthored HB3399 banning gender interventions for minors (parental-protection on minor gender care).

2.note: No specific CRT/SOGI/sex-ed curriculum vote separately cited.

3.note: Authored HB1443 (criminalizes child-like sex dolls) and HB2000 (sex-offender registration for grooming) — penalties for sexualized content targeting minors.

profile.religion: Lutheran (member, St. Paul's Evangelical Lutheran Church, Brenham)
profile.background: R senator SD-18 since 2014 (7 prior TX House terms from 2001). Chairs Senate Health & Human Services. TCU grad; former Washington County Chamber CEO.

lois-kolkhorst@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (autopilot). Strong social/cultural conservative: joint-author SB8 (Heartbeat Act), primary author SB6 (2017 'bathroom bill'), SB14 yea (trans-youth care ban), HB1927 yea (permitless carry; NRA/TSRA A+), SB1 yea (2021 election integrity), SB2 yea (2025 ESA/vouchers), author SB147 & SB17 (foreign-land-ownership bans). DOCUMENTED MODERATE FLAG: fiscal record — Texas Taxpayers Fiscal Index grade F (2023, 2013) — scored FALSE on balanced-budget cell. Texas Values 95% (Faith & Family Champion). Lutheran (St. Paul's, Brenham). Running 2026 (general vs Erica Gillum D).

0.note: Joint-author of SB8 (Texas Heartbeat Act); authored Born-Alive Infant Protection Act; Texas Right to Life Legislator of the Year.

profile.background: R representative HD-19 since 2023; former Austin City Council member (D8, 2015-19) and TPPF senior fellow on taxes/spending. Fiscal/limited-government conservative.

ellen-troxclair@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (autopilot). Strong conservative across life/marriage/family/guns/fiscal. AUTHORED HB229 'Women's Bill of Rights / What is a Woman Act' (signed 2025); yes on SB1257 (detransitioners), SB2 (vouchers), SB10/SB11/SB965 (religious liberty), HB796 (TX Sovereignty Act); no on HB2 ($8.5B school spending, fiscal restraint); authored proof-of-citizenship voter bill; NRA-PVF + GOA endorsed. TAL 97% pro-life; Texas Values 100% Champion; Texas Freedom Index 55%. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (noted, not scored as FALSE — tactical): voted NO on HJR98 (Article V Convention of States). No church found. Running 2026 (general opponent withdrew).

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 97%; yes on SB8 (Women's Privacy Act), SB33 (no taxpayer-funded abortion travel).

1.note: Pro-life voting slate under TX near-total ban; SB33, SB1233.

profile.background: D senator SD-19 (San Antonio/Uvalde) since 2021; prior TX House 2008-21 and San Antonio City Council. Attorney. Ran for US Senate 2024 (lost Dem primary to Allred). Leading post-Uvalde gun-control advocate.

roland-gutierrez@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (autopilot). Consistently progressive on rubric issues; no documented conservative cross-party deviation. Voted NO on SB8 and abortion restrictions, authored pro-access bills (SB122/123/227), PP-endorsed; co-filed SB150 (SOGI anti-discrimination); opposed SB2 vouchers; blasted Operation Lone Star ('waste of billions'); leading gun-control author (SB144 red-flag, raise-age) and opposed HB1927 permitless carry; filed SB575 to end police qualified immunity. ONE DOCUMENTED CREDIT: SB146 (~$300M Uvalde victim-compensation fund) scored TRUE on victim-support cell. Texas Taxpayers fiscal grade F; AFL-CIO/PP-endorsed. Still SD-19, on 2026 Dem primary ballot.

0.note: Voted NO on SB8 and a string of abortion restrictions; authored pro-access exception/decriminalization bills.

1.note: Authored SB122/SB123 (exceptions) and SB227 (bars prosecution of pregnant individuals) — opposite of abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood (2022); abortion-industry support.

4.note: Co-filed SB150 promoting SOGI protections in policy.

profile.background: D senator SD-20 (Rio Grande Valley/McAllen) since 2003; prior TX House from 1981. Marine Corps Vietnam veteran; attorney (Georgetown JD); Vice Chair of Senate Finance. Reelected 2024 — seat next up 2028.

juan-chuy-hinojosa@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (autopilot). CANDIDACY CORRECTION: NOT retiring in 2026 — the 'not seeking reelection' story is mistaken identity with former U.S. Rep. Ruben Hinojosa (TX-15). Sen. Juan 'Chuy' Hinojosa won reelection 2024; term runs to Jan 2029; seat next up 2028 (set candidacy_status: incumbent). Record is consistently progressive on rubric issues despite South-TX-Dem reputation: voted NO on SB8 (2021) and the 2011 sonogram bill; authored civil-union (2013) and same-sex-marriage-ban-repeal bills, public marriage-equality letter; voted NO on SB14 (trans-care ban) and SB2 (vouchers); broke quorum and opposed SB7/SB1 election bills; voted NO on HB1927 permitless carry; argued federal preemption against SB4 border law. JBS Freedom Index 17% (2021). Conservative-coded votes noted (HB492 no-knock limits yea, HB3 Pandemic Response Act yea) do not map to rubric TRUE cells. No church affiliation confirmed.

0.note: Voted NO on SB8 (2021 Heartbeat Act) and AGAINST the 2011 sonogram bill (not among the pro-life Dems Uresti/Lucio/Zaffirini).

1.note: Anti-restriction abortion record; opposite of abolition.

4.note: No PP/NARAL/EMILY's List rating or money located either direction.

0.note: Authored civil-union bill (2013) and same-sex-marriage-ban repeal; public marriage-equality letter (2014).

1.note: Authored repeal of TX same-sex-marriage ban.

2.note: Voted NO on SB14 (2023 gender-transition-care ban for minors; final passage 19-12 party-line).

4.note: Listed among Equality Texas 'Top 5' senators on LGBT issues.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/vouchers): 'public tax dollars should be dedicated to our Pre-K-12 public school system.'

1.note: No parental-consent vote separately cited.

0.note: No religious-liberty vote found.

1.note: No conscience-exemption vote found.

0.note: No CBDC position found.

3.note: Opposed SB7/SB1 election bills that restricted mail-in voting and drop boxes.

0.note: Criminal-justice-reform orientation (Fair Defense Act, DNA testing); no affirmative pro-police or defund vote to score.
